html:
City Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Minutes of the meeting of the Stockholm City Council held on Monday, June 15, 2026. Time: 2:00 PM - 6:38 PM Location: Council Chamber Present: See appendix §§1-17, §§19-34

Approved on June 22, 2026 Certified on June 23, 2026

Olle Burell Cecilia Brinck Sara Stenudd

Karin Jöback Secretary

Page 1 (52)

City Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 § 1 Question Time

Question 1 Councilor Fjellner (M) asked a question about the traffic situation in Stockholm.

Councilor Strömgren (MP) answered the question.

Question 2 Emelie Wassermann (SD) asked a question about whether the city council believes that the residents of Stockholm have been misled about the future of the Svedmyrabad swimming pool.

Councilor Valeskog (S) answered the question.

Question 3 Councilor Jönsson (L) asked a question about how the city council intends to proceed with the Eolshäll scandal.

Councilor Ojanne (S) answered the question.

Question 4 Jonas Naddebo (C) asked a question about the most vulnerable members of society.

Councilor Ojanne (S) answered the question.

Question 5 Nike Örbrink (KD) asked a question about the chaos on Sveavägen.

Councilor Strömgren (MP) answered the question.

Question 6 Councilor Hedin (M) asked a question about students being allowed to take digital devices home during the summer vacation.

Councilor Bjuggren (S) answered the question.

Question 7 Councilor Jönsson (L) asked a question about how the living conditions for residents of Skärholmen will be improved.

Councilor Wanngård (S) answered the question.

Question 8 Johan Kling (C) asked a question about supervision following the removal of the food requirement.

Councilor Ojanne (S) answered the question. Page 2 (52)

City Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15

Page 3 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 § 2 The meeting is opened.

Item 24 will be discussed after item 30, as announced.

The Municipal Council will set aside a portion of the meeting to honor Jan Jönsson (L), the outgoing group leader. The ceremony will take place immediately before item 24, as announced.

Page 4 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 § 3 Attendance

Attendance details are listed in Appendix 1.

Page 5 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 § 4 Review

The First Vice-Chair, Cecilia Brinck (M), and the Second Vice-Chair, Sara Stenudd (V), will review the minutes of today's meeting together with the Chair, Olle Burell (S). The review will take place on June 22, 2026.

Page 6 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 § 5 Items for consideration by the Municipal Council KS 2026/13

Decision The following items are submitted to the Municipal Council and are recorded in the minutes:

Report on decisions not implemented according to the Act (1993:387) on Support and Services for Certain Individuals with Disabilities (LSS) and the Social Services Act (SoL), for the first quarter of 2026. KS 2026/737

Submitted documents for consideration by the Municipal Council, from May 21, 2026, to June 10, 2026.

Administrative Court of Stockholm Judgment (2026-05-22) regarding the Municipal Council's decision of May 26, 2025, § 17, concerning the legality of a proposal for a fee according to the Alcohol Act and the sales hours for farm sales. The Administrative Court upholds the appeals and annuls point 4 of the challenged decision. Case No. 10986-25. KS 2025/834-14

Page 7 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Item 6 Election Matters in the Municipal Council KS 2026/9

Decision

  1. Resignations and dismissals are approved.
  2. Elections will be held to appoint a new person to the position, according to the election committee's proposal.
  3. Unless otherwise stated, the most recently elected substitute will serve last in order of seniority for their party.
  4. If a substitute/alternate is appointed to a regular council member position, a new substitute/alternate will be appointed.
  5. For resignations from the municipal council, a new official count is requested from the regional administrative board.
  6. Vacancies are postponed for further consideration.

Subject The election matters for the municipal council on June 15, 2026, are being addressed.

Decision-Making Process The chairperson notes that there is a proposed decision, which is the proposal from the municipal board. The chairperson determines that the municipal council will make a decision based on the municipal board's proposal.

Page 8 (52)

City Council Meeting Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Item 7 Guidelines for Child Safety in the Municipal Preschools of the City of Stockholm Proposal from the Preschool Committee Reference Number KS 2026/321

Decision

  1. The Guidelines for Child Safety in the municipal preschools of the City of Stockholm, attached as Appendix 1 to the report, are approved.
  2. "Safe Preschool - Guidelines for Child Safety in the municipal preschools of the City of Stockholm," reference number KS 2021/1321, is hereby repealed.

Dissenting Opinions All members of the Moderate Party, the Liberal Party, and the Center Party object to the decision and vote in favor of their own proposal. Subject Matter The report from the City Executive Board is considered. The report concerns the Guidelines for Child Safety in the municipal preschools of the City of Stockholm. The debate is documented in Appendix 2.

Proposed Decisions Councilor Mattsson (Social Democrats) proposes that the City Council adopt the decision as proposed by the City Executive Board. Sophia Granswed Baat (Moderate Party), Isabel Smedberg-Palmqvist (Liberal Party), and Ismail Ali (Center Party) propose that the City Council adopt the decision as proposed by the Moderate Party, the Liberal Party, and the Center Party in the City Executive Board.

Decision Outcome The Chair puts the proposals to a vote and determines that the City Council adopts the decision as proposed by the City Executive Board.

Page 9 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Item 8 New Maximum Fee Rates for 2026 – Revision of Fees for Stockholm City's Activities in Preschool, After-School Care, and Care Services During Unconventional Working Hours, Regardless of Legal Form KS 2026/279

Decision

  1. The revised fee levels for Stockholm City's activities in preschool, after-school care, and care services during unconventional working hours, regardless of legal form, are approved according to the details outlined in the report, provided that:

a) The government decides on the announced change to the regulation (2001:160) regarding state subsidies to municipalities that apply maximum fee rates in preschool and after-school care, and that: b) The Swedish National Agency for Education decides on new fee levels starting July 1, 2026, in accordance with preliminary announcements.

  1. The revised fee levels for Stockholm City's activities in preschool, after-school care, and care services during unconventional working hours, regardless of legal form, will be effective from July 1, 2026, provided that the conditions in item 1 a-b above are met.

The Matter The Municipal Board's report is considered.

The report concerns the new maximum fee rates for 2026 – revision of fees for Stockholm City's activities in preschool, after-school care, and care services during unconventional working hours, regardless of legal form.

Decision-Making Process The chairperson notes that there is a proposed decision, which is the Municipal Board's proposal. The chairperson determines that the Municipal Council will make a decision based on the Municipal Board's proposal.

Page 10 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Item 9 Action Plan for Children's Rights 2030 Reference: KS 2025/1335

Decision

  1. The Action Plan for Children's Rights 2030, as detailed in Appendix 2 of the report, is approved.
  2. Stockholms Stadshus AB is requested to adopt the Action Plan for Children's Rights 2030 for its own operations, and to instruct all board members within the group to also adopt the Action Plan for Children's Rights 2030, as detailed in Appendix 2 of the report.
  3. The Program for Children's Rights and Influence in the City of Stockholm 2018 – 2022 (reference number 171–1526/2016) is hereby discontinued.

Dissenting Opinions All members of the Moderate Party expressed dissent from the decision, supporting their own proposal instead. All members of the Sweden Democrats expressed dissent from the decision, supporting their own proposal instead. All members of the Liberals and the Center Party expressed dissent from the decision, supporting their own proposal instead.

Subject Matter The report from the Municipal Board is being considered. The report concerns the Action Plan for Children's Rights 2030. Statements made during the debate are included in Appendix 2.

Proposed Decisions Councilor Lindhagen (Green Party) proposes that the Municipal Council adopt the decision as proposed by the Municipal Board. Annika Elmlund (Moderate Party) proposes that the Municipal Council adopt the decision as proposed by the Moderate Party in the Municipal Board. Gabriel Kroon (Sweden Democrats) proposes that the Municipal Council adopt the decision as proposed by the Sweden Democrats, as detailed in Appendix 3. Councilor Jönsson (Liberals) and Johan Kling (Center Party) propose that the Municipal Council adopt the decision as proposed by the Liberals and the Center Party in the Municipal Board, which primarily entails... Page 11 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Regarding a referral back to committee, and secondly, that the Municipal Council makes a decision according to the proposal from the Liberal and Center parties.

Order of Decisions The chairperson assesses the proposal for referral, considering whether the matter should be decided today, and determines that the Municipal Council decides that the matter will be decided today.

The chairperson then compares the proposals and determines that the Municipal Council makes a decision according to the proposal from the Municipal Board.

Page 12 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Item 10 Action Plan for an Equal Stockholm 2030 Reference: KS 2025/1616

Decision

  1. The Action Plan for an Equal Stockholm 2030, attached as Appendix 2 to the report, is approved.
  2. Stockholms Stadshus AB is requested to adopt the Action Plan for an Equal Stockholm 2030, as detailed in Appendix 2 of the report, for its own operations, and to instruct all board members within the group to do the same.
  3. The program for an equal Stockholm 2018–2022 (reference number 434-21/2017) is hereby discontinued.

Dissenting Opinions All members of the Moderate Party object to the decision in favor of their own proposal. All members of the Sweden Democrats object to the decision in favor of their own proposal. All members of the Liberals and the Center Party object to the decision in favor of their own proposal. All members of the Christian Democrats object to the decision in favor of their own proposal.

Subject Matter The report from the Municipal Executive Board is being considered. The report concerns the Action Plan for an Equal Stockholm 2030. For comments made during the debate, see Appendix 2.

Proposed Decisions The Mayor Lindhagen (Green Party) proposes that the Municipal Council adopt the decision as proposed by the Municipal Executive Board. Annika Elmlund (Moderate Party) proposes that the Municipal Council adopt the decision as proposed by the Moderate Party in the Municipal Executive Board. Gabriel Kroon (Sweden Democrats) proposes that the Municipal Council adopt the decision as proposed by the Sweden Democrats, see Appendix 3.

Page 13 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15

Councilor Jönsson (L) and Johan Kling (C) propose that the municipal council decide according to the proposal from the Liberals and the Center Party, which primarily involves referring the matter back for reconsideration, and secondarily, that the municipal council decide according to the proposal from the Liberals and the Center Party.

Nike Örbrink (KD) proposes that the municipal council decide according to the proposal from the Christian Democrats, as presented to the municipal board.

Order of Decisions The chairperson assesses the proposal for referral, considering whether the matter should be decided today, and determines that the municipal council decides that the matter should be decided today.

The chairperson then compares the proposals and determines that the municipal council decides according to the municipal board's proposal.

Page 14 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Agenda Item 11 Policy Decision for the "New Swimming and Sports Facility" Project in Västertorp, property Isprinsessan 5 Proposal from the Property Committee and the Sports Committee KS 2026/504

Decision

  1. The policy direction for the "New Swimming and Sports Facility" project in Västertorp, property Isprinsessan 5, in accordance with the Property Committee's proposal, is approved.
  2. The Property Committee is authorized to continue planning the conditions for the "New Swimming and Sports Facility" project in Västertorp, Isprinsessan 5, with a project cost as detailed in the appendix to the report, as a basis for a future implementation decision.
  3. The expenses for 2026 shall be included within the committee's investment budget. Expenses for future years will be addressed in the 2027 budget.

Dissenting Opinions All members of the Liberal Party, the Center Party, and the Christian Democrats object to the decision in favor of their own proposal.

The Matter The Municipal Board's report is considered. The report concerns the policy decision for the "New Swimming and Sports Facility" project in Västertorp, property Isprinsessan 5. Statements made during the debate are included in Appendix 2.

Proposed Decisions Maria-Elsa Salvo (S) proposes that the Municipal Council decide according to the Municipal Board's proposal. Peter Öberg (L), Jonas Naddebo (C), and Mikael Valier Furtenbach (KD) propose that the Municipal Council decide according to the Liberal Party, the Center Party, and the Christian Democrats' proposed decision presented to the Municipal Board. Page 15 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15

Decision Process The chairperson puts the proposals to a vote and determines that the Municipal Council decides according to the Municipal Board's proposal.

Page 16 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Agenda Item 12 Revised guidelines for the project "Renovation and Development of Kampementsbadet," part of the property Norra Djurgården 1:1. Proposal from the Property Committee and the Sports Committee KS 2026/505

Decision

  1. The revised guidelines for the project "Renovation and Development of Kampementsbadet" are approved.
  2. The Property Committee is authorized to continue planning for the project "Renovation and Development of Kampementsbadet," in accordance with option C, as a basis for a future implementation decision, with an investment cost as detailed in the appendix to the report.
  3. The expenses for the year 2026 must be accommodated within the committee's investment budget. Expenses for future years will be addressed in the budget for 2027.

Dissenting Opinions All members of the Moderate Party, the Liberal Party, the Center Party, and the Christian Democrats expressed dissent from the decision, supporting their own proposal instead.

The Matter The report from the Municipal Board is considered. The report concerns the revised guidelines for the project "Renovation and Development of Kampementsbadet," part of the property Norra Djurgården 1:1. For comments during the debate, see Appendix 2.

Proposed Decision Councilor Mattsson (V) proposes that the Municipal Council adopt the decision as proposed by the Municipal Board. Tom Hedrup (M), Peter Öberg (L), Jonas Naddebo (C), and Mikael Valier Furtenbach (KD) propose that the Municipal Council adopt the decision as proposed by the Moderate Party, the Liberal Party, the Center Party, and the Christian Democrats in the Municipal Board. Page 17 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15

Order of Decisions The chairperson presents the proposals and determines that the Municipal Council adopts the decision as proposed by the Municipal Board.

Page 18 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Item 13 Implementation Decision for the "Temporary Hall" Project at Dalhagen Football Field, part of the property Akalla 4:1. Proposal from the Property Committee and the Sports Committee KS 2026/506

Decision

  1. The Property Committee is granted the right to implement the "Temporary Hall" project at Dalhagen Football Field, part of the property Akalla 4:1, with a project cost as detailed in the appendix to the report.
  2. The expenses for the year 2026 must be covered within the committee's investment budget. Expenses for future years will be addressed in the 2027 budget.

The Matter The Municipal Board's report is considered.

The report concerns the Implementation Decision for the "Temporary Hall" project at Dalhagen Football Field, part of the property Akalla 4:1.

Statements made during the debate are included in Appendix 2.

Proposed Decision Elvir Kazinic (S) proposes that the Municipal Council make a decision according to the Municipal Board's proposal.

Decision Procedure The Chair notes that there is a proposed decision, which is the Municipal Board's proposal. The Chair determines that the Municipal Council will make a decision according to the Municipal Board's proposal.

Page 19 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Item 14 Activity Requirements for Recipients of Social Assistance - Responsibility for Providing Activities in the City of Stockholm Proposal from the Labor Market Committee and the Social Services Committee KS 2026/416

Decision

  1. The Labor Market Committee shall provide activities that meet the activity requirements as stipulated in Section 12, Chapter 6a of the Social Services Act (SoL).
  2. This decision is valid provided that the Parliament makes a decision regarding activity requirements for recipients of social assistance in accordance with proposal 2025/26:207, "Activity Requirements for Recipients of Social Assistance."

The Matter The statement from the Municipal Board is considered.

The statement concerns "Activity Requirements for Recipients of Social Assistance - Responsibility for Providing Activities in the City of Stockholm."

Statements made during the debate are included in Appendix 2.

Proposed Decision Arvid Vikman Rindevall (S) proposes that the Municipal Council decides according to the proposal from the Municipal Board.

Decision Procedure The Chair notes that there is a proposed decision, which is the proposal from the Municipal Board. The Chair determines that the Municipal Council will decide according to the proposal from the Municipal Board.

Page 20 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Item 15 Guidelines for the provision of specialized housing under the Social Services Act (SoL) and the Support for People with Disabilities Act (LSS), including psychosocial supported housing. Proposal from the Social Services Committee KS 2026/146

Decision

  1. The guidelines for the provision of specialized housing under the Social Services Act (SoL) and the Support for People with Disabilities Act (LSS), including psychosocial supported housing, as outlined in the appendix to the report, are approved.
  2. The Social Services Committee shall develop implementation guidelines for these guidelines.

The Matter The report from the Municipal Board is considered.

The report concerns the Guidelines for the provision of specialized housing under the Social Services Act (SoL) and the Support for People with Disabilities Act (LSS), including psychosocial supported housing.

Decision-Making Procedure The chairperson states that there is a proposed decision, which is the proposal from the Municipal Board. The chairperson determines that the Municipal Council will make a decision based on the proposal from the Municipal Board.

Page 21 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Item 16 Guidelines for the Handling of Financial Assistance in the City of Stockholm Proposal from the Social Welfare Committee KS 2026/264

Decision

  1. The Guidelines for the Handling of Financial Assistance in the City of Stockholm, attached as an appendix to the report, are approved.
  2. The Guidelines for the Handling of Financial Assistance, case number 150-2129/2016, are hereby repealed.

Dissenting Opinions All members of the Moderate Party and the Christian Democrats expressed dissent from the decision, in favor of their own proposal. All members of the Sweden Democrats expressed dissent from the decision, in favor of their own proposal. All members of the Liberals and the Center Party expressed dissent from the decision, in favor of their own proposal.

The Matter The report from the Municipal Board was considered. The report concerned the Guidelines for the Handling of Financial Assistance in the City of Stockholm. Statements made during the debate are included in Appendix 2.

Proposed Decisions Rashid Mohammed (V) proposed that the Municipal Council adopt the decision as proposed by the Municipal Board. Councilor Hedin (M) and Nike Örbrink (KD) proposed that the Municipal Council adopt the decision as proposed by the Moderate Party and the Christian Democrats in the Municipal Board. Gabriel Kroon (SD) proposed that the Municipal Council adopt the decision as proposed by the Sweden Democrats in the Municipal Board. Anne-Lie Elfvén (L) and Claes Nyberg (C) proposed that the Municipal Council adopt the decision as proposed by the Liberals and the Center Party in the Municipal Board. Page 22 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15

Order of Decision The Chair put the proposals to a vote and determined that the Municipal Council adopted the decision as proposed by the Municipal Board.

Page 23 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Item 17 Supplementary agreement to the development agreement, including the transfer of land and the granting of leasehold rights for residential and office buildings, within a portion of property Årsta 1:1 and property Postgården 7, located in Årstafältet. Proposal from the development committee KS 2026/454

Decision The supplementary agreement, as detailed in the attachment to the report, is approved. The development committee is authorized to enter into a supplementary agreement to the development agreement, including the transfer of land for office buildings and the granting of leasehold rights for residential buildings, within a portion of property Årsta 1:1 and property Postgården 7, located in Årstafältet, with a change of contracting parties and security arrangements.

The Matter The municipal board's report is considered.

The report concerns the supplementary agreement to the development agreement, including the transfer of land and the granting of leasehold rights for residential and office buildings, within a portion of property Årsta 1:1 and property Postgården 7, located in Årstafältet.

Decision-Making Procedure The chairperson notes that there is a proposal for a decision, which is the municipal board's proposal. The chairperson determines that the municipal council will make a decision based on the municipal board's proposal.

Page 24 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Item 19 St Erik Markutveckling AB's acquisition of all shares in Nya Lagerbolaget Y (Warehouse 5) from Stockholms Hamn AB. Initiative from Stockholms Stadshus AB Reference: KS 2026/456

Decision:

  1. The acquisition by Stockholms Hamn AB of two newly formed limited liability companies, one a holding company ("Nya Holdingbolaget X AB") and one a subsidiary ("Nya Lagerbolaget Y AB"), for the purpose of transferring ownership of the building Warehouse 5 to St Erik Markutveckling AB, or a wholly-owned subsidiary within St Erik Markutveckling AB's group, is approved.
  2. The acquisition by St Erik Markutveckling AB or a wholly-owned subsidiary, and the sale by Stockholms Hamn AB of all shares in Nya Lagerbolaget Y AB, thereby transferring ownership of Warehouse 5 in Frihamnen, for a purchase price estimated at a preliminary market value of approximately 400 million Swedish krona, primarily in accordance with the share transfer agreement between Nya Holdingbolaget X AB (a wholly-owned subsidiary of Stockholms Hamn AB) and St Erik Markutveckling AB, as detailed in Appendix 5 of the report, is approved.
  3. The following individuals are nominated as board members, deputy board members, lay auditors, and deputy lay auditors for Nya Holdingbolaget X AB and Nya Lagerbolaget Y AB, respectively, until the next annual general meeting in 2027, as listed in Appendix 6 of the report.
  4. The new articles of association for Nya Holdingbolaget X AB and Nya Lagerbolaget Y AB, as detailed in Appendix 7 of the report, are approved.
  5. Stockholms Hamn AB's decision to, at an appropriate time after the transfer of shares in Nya Lagerbolaget Y AB, as described in item 2 above, liquidate the company Nya Holdingbolaget X AB, is approved.

The Matter: The report from the Municipal Board is reviewed.

The report concerns St Erik Markutveckling AB's acquisition of all shares in Nya Lagerbolaget Y (Warehouse 5) from Stockholms Hamn AB. Page 25 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Decision-Making Procedure The chairperson states that there is a proposal for a decision, which is the proposal from the municipal board. The chairperson determines that the municipal council will make a decision based on the proposal from the municipal board.

Page 26 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 § 20 Letter of Intent between the Planning and Development Committee and Stockholms Hamn AB regarding ship traffic on Värtapiren and Frihamnspiren, future land use in southern Norra Djurgårdsstaden, land transfer, and enhanced electrification of Frihamnspiren. Reference: KS 2026/460

Decision The Letter of Intent between the Planning and Development Committee and Stockholms Hamn AB regarding ship traffic on Värtapiren and Frihamnspiren, future land use in southern Norra Djurgårdsstaden, land transfer, and enhanced electrification of Frihamnspiren, as detailed in Appendix 5 of the report, is approved.

Dissenting Opinions All members of the Center Party expressed dissent from the decision, supporting their own proposed alternative.

Background The report from the municipal board is considered. The report concerns the Letter of Intent between the Planning and Development Committee and Stockholms Hamn AB regarding ship traffic on Värtapiren and Frihamnspiren, future land use in southern Norra Djurgårdsstaden, land transfer, and enhanced electrification of Frihamnspiren. For comments made during the debate, see Appendix 2.

Proposed Decision Svante Linusson (C) proposed that the municipal council should make a decision based on the Center Party's proposed decision from the municipal board.

Decision-Making Procedure The chairperson compared the proposals and determined that the municipal council would make a decision based on the proposal from the municipal board.

Page 27 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Item 21 Revised implementation decision and additions to agreements regarding the development of the "Blackebergs Torg" project within the property Norrmannen 11, etc. Proposal from the Development Committee KS 2026/562

Decision

  1. The Development Committee is granted the right to proceed with the development within the property Norrmannen 11, etc., for the "Blackebergs Torg" project, with a project cost of 79.0 million SEK, a project income of 1.1 million SEK, and a sales income of 38.7 million SEK.
  2. The expenses for the year 2026 must be within the committee's investment budget. Expenses for future years will be addressed in the budget for the year 2027.

Dissenting Opinions All members of the Sweden Democrats party object to the decision in favor of their own proposal.

The Matter The statement from the Municipal Board is considered. The statement concerns the Revised implementation decision and additions to agreements regarding the development of the "Blackebergs Torg" project within the property Norrmannen 11, etc. Statements made during the debate are included in Appendix 2.

Proposed Decision Gabriel Kroon (SD) proposes that the Municipal Council decide according to the Sweden Democrats' proposed decision, as outlined in Appendix 3.

Decision Process The Chair compares the proposals and determines that the Municipal Council will decide according to the Municipal Board's proposal.

Page 28 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Item 22 Revised implementation decision for the project Vårbergsvägen, within the Fokus Skärholmen program. Proposal from the Planning and Development Committee Reference: KS 2026/564

Decision

  1. The Planning and Development Committee is authorized to proceed with further investigations and design work for the Vårbergsvägen project, and to implement Phase 1, with a total project expenditure of 123.7 million SEK and a project income of 7 million SEK. This is subject to a future revised implementation decision that will include Phase 2 when the feasibility conditions for that phase have improved.
  2. The expenses for the year 2026 must be covered within the committee's investment budget. Expenses for subsequent years will be addressed in the budget for the year 2027.

Dissenting Opinions All members of the Sweden Democrats party object to the decision and vote in favor of their own proposal.

The Matter The statement from the Municipal Board is considered. The statement concerns the Revised Implementation Decision for the Vårbergsvägen project within the Fokus Skärholmen program. For comments made during the debate, see Appendix 2.

Proposed Decision Gabriel Kroon (SD) proposes that the Municipal Council adopt the decision as proposed by the Sweden Democrats party, as detailed in Appendix 3.

Decision Process The Chair compares the proposals and determines that the Municipal Council adopts the proposal from the Municipal Board.

Page 29 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Item 23 Amendment to the General Local Regulations for the Municipality of Stockholm regarding the closure of the dog park in Trekanten, within the Hägersten-Älvsjö district administration area. Proposal from the Hägersten-Älvsjö District Administration Board KS 2026/429

Decision

  1. The amendments to the General Local Regulations for the Municipality of Stockholm, in accordance with the proposal of the Hägersten-Älvsjö District Administration Board and Appendix 1 to the report, are approved.
  2. The decision is reported to the County Administrative Board of Stockholm.
  3. The General Local Regulations for the Municipality of Stockholm will be published on the municipality's digital notice board after the County Administrative Board of Stockholm has approved the decision.
  4. The decision takes effect from the date of publication.

Dissenting Opinions All members of the Center Party and the Christian Democrats object to the decision in favor of their own proposal.

The Matter The report from the Municipal Board is considered. The report concerns the amendment to the General Local Regulations for the Municipality of Stockholm regarding the closure of the dog park in Trekanten, within the Hägersten-Älvsjö district administration area. The debate is recorded in Appendix 2.

Proposed Decision Jonas Naddebo (C) and Nike Örbrink (KD) propose that the municipal council decide according to the proposal from the Center Party and the Christian Democrats, as presented to the Municipal Board.

Decision-Making Process The chairperson puts the proposals to a vote and determines that the municipal council decides according to the proposal from the Municipal Board.

Page 30 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Item 24 Amendment to the General Local Regulations for the Municipality of Stockholm, concerning dog exercise areas and dog parks within the Skarpnäck district administration area, Nytorps field. Proposal from the Skarpnäck District Administration Board KS 2024/756

Decision

  1. The amendments to the General Local Regulations for the Municipality of Stockholm, in accordance with the proposal from the Skarpnäck District Administration Board and attachments 1 and 2 to the report, are approved.
  2. The decision is reported to the County Administrative Board of Stockholm.
  3. The General Local Regulations for the Municipality of Stockholm will be published on the municipality's online notice board after the County Administrative Board of Stockholm has approved the decision.
  4. The decision takes effect from the date of publication.

The Matter The report from the Municipal Board is considered.

The report concerns the amendment to the General Local Regulations for the Municipality of Stockholm, concerning dog exercise areas and dog parks within the Skarpnäck district administration area, Nytorps field.

Statements made during the debate are included in attachment 2.

Decision-making process The chairperson states that there is a proposed decision, which is the proposal from the Municipal Board. The chairperson determines that the Municipal Council will make a decision based on the proposal from the Municipal Board.

Page 31 (52)

City Council Meeting Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Agenda Item 25 Transition to Digital Personnel Records for the Municipal Boards of the City of Stockholm Reference: KS 2026/153

Decision

  1. The municipal boards of the City of Stockholm shall transition to digital personnel records, in accordance with the report.
  2. The municipal boards of the City of Stockholm shall scan existing paper-based personnel records to create a comprehensive digital personnel record.
  3. The City Executive Board, in collaboration with the Culture Committee (the city archive), shall lead the work of transitioning to digital personnel records, in accordance with the report.
  4. The City Executive Board is responsible for system administration, contract management, and development.
  5. The City Executive Board is granted funding of 70 million SEK for the development and implementation of digital personnel records for the years 2026-2031. Funding will be provided through the annually allocated funds for IT development costs in the central reserve, in accordance with the report.

Subject The report from the City Executive Board is considered.

The report concerns the transition to digital personnel records for the municipal boards of the City of Stockholm.

Statements made during the debate are included in Appendix 2.

Decision-Making Process The chairperson notes that there is a proposed decision, which is the proposal from the City Executive Board. The chairperson determines that the City Council will make a decision based on the proposal from the City Executive Board.

Page 32 (52)

Municipal Council Minutes No. 7 2026-06-15 Item 26 Approval of amendments to the articles of association of the "Elektronikcentrum" foundation and nomination of a member to the board. KS 2026/611

Decision

  1. The amendments to the articles of association of the "Elektronikcentrum" foundation are approved, as detailed in the report.
  2. Karin Wanngård is nominated as a member of the board of the "Elektronikcentrum" foundation, serving until the first meeting in 2028.

Appendix 3

Municipal Council Proposed Resolution 2026-06-15 Item 9 Reference Number KS 2025/1335

Action Plan for Children's Rights 2030

Proposed Resolution

  1. The Municipal Council rejects the proposal for a resolution submitted by the Municipal Board.
  1. Furthermore, the following is noted:

The Sweden Democrats share the view that children's rights are fundamental and should be upheld in all municipal activities. For this very reason, the work should be based on what is stipulated by law. The UN Convention on the Rights of the Child has been Swedish law since 2020 and is therefore binding on the City of Stockholm and all its committees.

Therefore, there is no need for an additional municipal action plan that risks leading to more administration or opening the door to interpretations that go beyond the actual content of the convention.

The city already has a large number of governing documents, programs, and strategies that are to be implemented in the various operations. The school's mandate is clearly regulated by law, curriculum, and national governing documents. Introducing yet another overarching action plan carries the risk of double governance and unclear allocation of responsibilities. Every hour spent on new reporting requirements is an hour less spent with children.

The UN Convention on the Rights of the Child should be applied as it was adopted, not through local interpretations that risk driving the formation of norms or ideological perspectives beyond the intentions of the law.

Gabriel Kroon (Sweden Democrats)

Linnea Vinge (Sweden Democrats)

Page 47 (52)

Appendix 3

City Council Proposed Resolution 2026-06-15 Item 10 Reference Number KS 2025/1616

Action Plan for an Equal Stockholm 2030

Proposed Resolution

  1. The City Council rejects the proposal for a resolution submitted by the City Executive Board.
  1. Furthermore, the following is noted:

The Sweden Democrats share the goal of a society where all people are treated equally and given good opportunities to shape their lives. Equality between women and men is a fundamental principle that must be upheld.

At the same time, we would like to raise certain objections to the proposed action plan.

The plan is largely based on a specific ideological problem description, where concepts such as gender power structures and critical analysis of norms are given a central role. We believe that the city's governing documents should primarily be based on an objective and pragmatic approach, with a focus on concrete problems and solutions rather than theoretical starting points.

We also see a risk that an excessive focus on gender and group affiliation will lead to the individual perspective being overshadowed. Public services should primarily be based on the individual's needs and circumstances, not on categorizations. Furthermore, the action plan includes extensive requirements for analyses, follow-up, and critical analysis of norms in essentially all activities. This risks increasing administrative burden and diverting resources from core functions such as schools, elderly care, and efforts to promote safety.

The Sweden Democrats believe that the city's efforts should be concentrated on concrete measures that strengthen safety, improve school results, and ensure a well-functioning welfare system for all, regardless of gender.

Gabriel Kroon (SD)

Linnea Vinge (SD)

Page 48 (52)

Appendix 3

City Council Proposed Resolution 2026-06-15 Item 16 Reference Number KS 2026/264

Guidelines for the Handling of Financial Assistance in the City of Stockholm

Proposed Resolution

  1. The City Council partially approves the proposal for a decision by the City Executive Board.
  1. Furthermore, the following is noted:

We acknowledge that guidelines for the handling of financial assistance are necessary to ensure legal certainty and equality within the city. In principle, we believe that financial assistance should adhere to the basic level required by law.

However, in this case, there are three particularly noteworthy special provisions in the City of Stockholm:

  1. SL passes (public transportation passes) should be granted to all applicants without a needs assessment.
  2. Children in families residing illegally in Stockholm should be eligible for financial assistance, while adults should receive emergency assistance.
  3. Individuals registered for job placement services who are receiving financial assistance should be granted four weeks of leave during the summer with full pay.

We propose that the three provisions listed above be removed from the guidelines.

Gabriel Kroon (SD) Linnea Vinge (SD)

Page 49 (52)

Appendix 3

Municipal Council Proposed Resolution 2026-06-15 Item 21 Reference Number KS 2026/562

Revised implementation decision and additions to agreements regarding the development of the "Blackebergs Torg" project within the property Norrmannen 11, etc.

Proposed Resolution:

  1. The Municipal Council rejects the proposal for a decision from the Municipal Board.
  1. Furthermore, the following is noted:

The "Blackebergs Torg" project involves the implementation of the detailed plan for Vinjegatan, located near the property Norrmannen 11 and others, in the Blackeberg district. The detailed plan enables new construction adjacent to Blackebergsplan by straightening and shifting Vinjegatan in a southwest direction. This creates the conditions for a new block with more than 120 apartments, as well as premises for office and commercial purposes, including a new healthcare center.

The Sweden Democrats view positively the development of districts with services, safety, and well-functioning public spaces. However, we believe that the current direction, with additional rental apartments in Blackeberg, is not the right path forward for the area.

Against this background, the Sweden Democrats believe that this matter should be rejected.

Gabriel Kroon (SD)

Linnea Vinge (SD)

Proposed Resolution:

  1. The Municipal Council rejects the proposal from the Municipal Board.
  1. Furthermore, the following is noted:

This matter concerns a revised implementation decision for the Vårbergsvägen project within the Fokus Skärholmen program. The project is currently paused, but the proposal suggests that the first phase of the project, Phase 1, should proceed, while the remaining phases remain on hold.

The Sweden Democrats are partially positive about the project but advocate for a revision to preserve green spaces and, where possible, develop a row house area.

Gabriel Kroon (SD) Linnea Vinge (SD)

Municipal Council Proposed Resolution 2026-06-15 Item 29 Reference Number KS 2026/564

Policy Decision Regarding AB Stockholmshem's New Construction of Apartments within Property Esbjörn 1, in Solhem, Spånga.

The Sweden Democrats have not been involved in the preparation of this matter within the company AB Stockholmshem. Despite being the second-largest opposition party, we have not been granted access to the company's board activities. We cannot be expected to take responsibility for a matter in which we have not participated. Therefore, we propose that this matter be rejected.

tldr: The City of Stockholm has decided on new regulations regarding child safety in municipal preschools, as well as new fees for preschool, after-school care, and childcare services offered during inconvenient hours.
lattlast:
The City of Stockholm has decided on new guidelines to make municipal preschools safer for children. The old safety regulations are no longer in effect, and the new regulations replace them. Not all parties were happy with this decision, as members from the Moderate Party, the Liberals, and the Center Party voted against it and wanted a different proposal.

Changes have also been decided regarding fees for services provided by preschools, after-school programs, and care services that operate during inconvenient hours. This means that the cost of these services will be adjusted according to the new rates. This decision applies regardless of the type of organization providing the service, but is dependent on the government approving changes to the state subsidies.

During the meeting, several issues were discussed by politicians, including traffic conditions in Stockholm, the situation on Sveavägen, and how to improve life in Skärholmen. It was also announced that a ruling from the Stockholm Administrative Court of Appeal has overturned a previous decision regarding regulations for the sale of alcohol at farm shops.
